name: David H. Bailey
affiliation_listed_in_cover_letter: Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (retired); University of California, Davis (affiliated)

filter_2_1_identity_confirmation.actual_affiliation_per_fetch: Senior Scientist (retired), Computational Research Department, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. Page also self-describes 'leading figure in the field of high-performance scientific computing' and 'leading figure in the field of computational and experimental mathematics'.
filter_2_1_identity_confirmation.uc_davis_affiliation: not confirmed on personal homepage (last update 1 Jan 2026); operator may verify via UC Davis math directory if needed.

filter_A_exp_math_editorial_overlap.rationale: Direct fetch of https://www.tandfonline.com/journals/uexm20/editorial-board returned HTTP 403 (anti-bot). Wayback Machine has no cached snapshot. Secondary web_search for 'David Bailey' / 'David H. Bailey' on the 2024-2025 EM editorial board returned 'no explicit evidence' of board membership; the recent EM board 'reboot' was led by Al Kasprzyk (Editor-in-Chief), with Pieter Belmans joining as a new board member per his blog post.
filter_A_exp_math_editorial_overlap.first_pass_search_synthesis_error: An initial Bing-mediated synthesis listed 'David Bailey (Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory)' as an EM board member. A targeted follow-up search contradicted this and attributed the confusion to Bailey's separate, self-run experimentalmath.info site. The follow-up finding is the more reliable signal.
evidence_urls: https://www.experimentalmath.info/ (Bailey's personal site, NOT the journal)

filter_B_bbc_cluster.verdict: DEFINITIVE FAIL by direct cluster membership
filter_B_bbc_cluster.rationale: David H. Bailey IS the lead 'Bailey' in the named 'Bailey-Borwein-Crandall' coauthorship cluster as defined in the task ('{David Bailey, Jonathan Borwein, Richard Crandall, Helaman Ferguson, Simon Plouffe}'). He is the corresponding author on the Bailey-Borwein-Crandall 1997 paper 'On the Khinchin constant' which is the foundational reference for the Khinchin-constant content of the very manuscript under review (cited as ref [1] BaileyBorweinCrandall1997). He is also a coauthor of refs [2] BaileyBorweinPlouffe1997 and [3] BaileyBroadhurst2001. His personal homepage explicitly names Borwein, Crandall, and Plouffe as 'longtime collaborators'.

filter_C_recent_activity.rationale: Personal homepage indicates active blog (Math Scholar, Mathematical Investor) updated through Jan 2026; published a 2017 AMS Levi L. Conant Prize-winning paper; 600+ publications. Even in retirement, Bailey continues to produce expository and mathematical work in the relevant areas.
filter_C_recent_activity.relevant_publications_recent_24mo: Inferred active via blog and book activity; no peer-reviewed journal article in last 24mo confirmed via this vetting pass.

filter_D_geographic.set_level_diversity_contribution: USA — non-AU; satisfies set-level non-AU requirement on its own.

filter_E_prior_interaction_with_author.rationale: Papanokechi is pseudonymous with only Zenodo deposits (10.5281/zenodo.19562751, 10.5281/zenodo.20173677, 10.5281/zenodo.20173678) on the ORCID profile; no journal coauthorship trail. No Bailey acknowledgment in the AEAL framework or the Khinchin-signature bundle README. No public peer-review interaction known.

filter_F_baustms_fit.rationale: Senior figure in computational and experimental mathematics; multiple major awards (IEEE Sidney Fernbach 1993, ACM Gordon Bell 2008, MAA Chauvenet 1993, AMS Levi L. Conant 2017). No retraction flags. Would be a natural BAustMS reviewer for a paper in this area if not for the Filter B blocker.
filter_F_baustms_fit.notes: Filter F evaluation is essentially moot given the Filter B definitive FAIL.

summary: David H. Bailey is the most natural expert reviewer one could name for a Khinchin-signature PSLQ paper -- precisely because he is one of the authors of the foundational paper the manuscript cites. That same fact makes him ineligible under the task's Filter B (direct BBC cluster membership). Filters A, C, D, E, F all pass or probable-pass, but Filter B is dispositive.
